text: Handling deadlines is a crucial skill in both study and work. Effective time management is key: break your tasks into smaller steps and set mini-deadlines for each one. Prioritization helps you focus on the most important or urgent tasks first, so you avoid wasting time on less critical work. Scope management is equally important—if a project seems too large, discuss with your supervisor or team about adjusting its scale. Finally, communication is vital. If you foresee a delay, inform others early and propose a realistic new deadline. By combining these strategies, you can meet deadlines with less stress and better results.
